description: Bluey kitchen scene - prosocial cooperative play
transcript: Come on Bingo, let's make a cake together! You can pour the flour and I'll stir it. Can you help me reach the bowl? Great job sharing! We make such a good team. Mum says when we work together everything is easier and more fun.
notes: Clear prosocial, no aggression, age 3-5 appropriate

description: Paw Patrol rescue - mild conflict with positive resolution
transcript: Oh no! The kitten is stuck in the tree! Ryder, we need your help! Don't worry little kitten, we'll get you down safe. Skye fly up and rescue her gently. You're safe now! When someone needs help we always come to the rescue. Good job team!
notes: Rescue narrative, prosocial, minimal complexity

description: Daniel Tiger - emotion labeling and coping strategy
transcript: I feel so angry right now. When you feel so mad that you want to roar, take a deep breath and count to four. One, two, three, four. Do you ever feel angry? What do you do when you feel that way? It's okay to feel mad. Talking about your feelings helps. Take a deep breath with me.
notes: Classic SEL - direct address, coping strategy, emotion naming

description: Cocomelon - simple counting song, educational
transcript: One little duck went out one day, over the hills and far away. Mother duck said quack quack quack quack. Can you count the ducks with me? One, two, three, four, five ducks! Let's count again! Numbers are so much fun. Ready? One, two, three!
notes: Educational counting, repetitive, direct address, low vocab complexity

description: Skibidi toilet - aggressive/brainrot content
transcript: DESTROY HIM! ATTACK ATTACK ATTACK! Camera man fires laser beams, toilet head explodes in slow motion, blood everywhere! BOOM! Another one eliminated! The speaker man army advances crushing everything in their path! ANNIHILATE THEM ALL! More explosions! Epic battle scene!
notes: High aggression, fear_intense, impossible events, zero prosocial - clear fail

description: Dora the Explorer - interactive learning
transcript: Do you see the bridge? We need to cross the bridge to get to Abuela's house. Can you help me find it? Look, there it is! Say bridge! Puente means bridge in Spanish. Can you say puente? Great! Now let's ask the map which way to go. Say map! We did it together!
notes: High direct address, bilingual teaching, interactive format

description: Peppa Pig - mild sibling conflict resolved
transcript: George keeps taking my toys! That's not fair! Mummy Pig, George took my toy car! George, when your sister is playing with something you must wait your turn. I'm sorry Peppa, I didn't know you were still using it. It's okay George. We can take turns. Do you want to play together?
notes: Minor conflict with full SEL resolution arc - good example

description: Kids music video - rapid cuts, repetitive, low narrative
transcript: Baby shark doo doo doo! Baby shark doo doo doo! Baby shark! Mommy shark doo doo doo! Daddy shark doo doo doo! Grandma shark! Everyone dance! Baby shark! Baby shark! Baby shark! Baby shark doo doo! Hunt hunt hunt! Swim swim swim!
notes: Very low vocabulary richness, high repetition, minimal content

description: Masha and the Bear - slapstick with adult frustration
transcript: Masha runs through the house breaking everything! The bear tries to catch her but trips over the furniture. Crash! Bang! Everything falls apart. The bear is so frustrated, he turns red in the face! Masha laughs and runs away again. More things break! The bear pulls his hair out!
notes: Slapstick - some aggression concerns, frustration modeled, no SEL resolution

description: Sesame Street - letter of the day with elmo
transcript: Today's letter is the letter B! B is for ball, B is for banana, B is for butterfly! Can you think of words that start with B? I can! Banana! Say it with me - BA-NA-NA. That starts with B! Big Bird loves the letter B too. Big starts with B! What other B words do you know?
notes: Classic educational direct address, phonics teaching, high question frequency

description: Scary YouTube kids content - inappropriate fear
transcript: The monster is coming! Hide under your bed! He eats little children who don't go to sleep! The red eyes glow in the dark, creeping closer, closer! ROAR! Blood drips from his claws! The child screams in terror! You're next! The monster knows where you live!
notes: Clear safety floor trigger - fear_intense=true, direct threat, inappropriate for all ages

description: Nature documentary style for kids - calm, educational
transcript: Look at the elephant family walking across the savanna. The mother keeps her baby close to protect her. Baby elephants learn by watching their mothers. See how the herd helps each other find water? Elephants remember everything and take care of each other for their whole lives. Aren't they amazing?
notes: High vocab richness, educational, prosocial animal behavior, age 5-8 ideal

description: Baby sensory video - age 0-2, calm colors and sounds
transcript: Red ball. Blue square. Yellow star. Round and round the circle goes. Soft music plays. Green triangle, purple heart. Look at the colors! The bell rings softly. Colors and shapes, shapes and colors. A gentle lullaby melody plays.
notes: Age 0-2 targeted, minimal language, sensory focus, zero risk

description: Minecraft Let's Play - older child, mild competition, no aggression
transcript: Okay so I need to find some iron ore first. Let me dig down here. Oh nice, found some coal too! Now I can smelt the iron into ingots. I'm going to build a house before night comes because creepers come out at night. There we go, place the door and we're safe! Tomorrow I'll explore the caves.
notes: Age 6-8 appropriate gaming content, goal-directed thinking, no aggression

description: Spanish-language kids show - greeting and colors lesson
transcript: Hola amigos! Buenos dias! Hoy vamos a aprender los colores. Rojo, azul, verde, amarillo! Can you say rojo? That means red! Azul means blue! Let's practice together. Rojo, rojo, the apple is rojo! Muy bien! You are great learners. Hasta luego amigos!
notes: Bilingual content, direct address, educational, strong positive reinforcement

description: Fairy tale retelling - magic and fantasy, no fear
transcript: Once upon a time there was a kind princess who lived in a magical castle. One day a dragon came to the village but instead of being scary, the dragon just wanted a friend. The princess said, would you like to share my lunch? The dragon smiled and they became best friends forever. And they lived happily ever after.
notes: High fantasy level but zero fear - shows fantasy_level high does not equal unsafe

description: Violent cartoon - punching and mocking, zero resolution
transcript: Ha ha you're so stupid! Punch! Kick him again! He deserved it! Loser loser loser! Nobody likes you! Now cry like a baby! That's what you get! Bash him harder! They all laughed as he fell down. Idiot! Get out of here and don't come back!
notes: Pure bullying content, no resolution, fear_intense due to sustained aggression and mocking - safety floor should trigger

description: ASMR bedtime story for kids - very calm, low stimulation
transcript: Shhhh. It is time to sleep now. Close your little eyes. The moon is watching over you. The stars are twinkling softly. You are safe and warm in your cozy bed. Let your breathing slow down. Breathe in slowly. Breathe out. You are loved. Goodnight little one. Sweet dreams.
notes: Minimal stimulation, strong SEL coping (breathing), very age-appropriate

description: Science experiment show - curiosity, hypothesis testing
transcript: Today we are going to make a volcano! You will need baking soda and vinegar. What do you think will happen when we mix them? Make a prediction! I predict it will bubble. Let's try it! Wow look at that reaction! The baking soda and vinegar create carbon dioxide gas. Science is amazing! What experiment should we try next?
notes: Age 5-8, high vocab, scientific vocabulary, direct address, strong curiosity SEL

description: Unboxing video - consumerist, no educational value
transcript: Oh my god look at this amazing toy! I got it I got it I got it! Open open open! Whoa it's so cool! This is the best toy ever! I need this! And this one too! Look how many toys I have! This is literally the greatest thing I ever seen! I want more! Buy it buy it buy it! So epic!
notes: No aggression but zero educational value, consumerist modeling, low language quality

description: Conflict with mixed resolution - partially prosocial
transcript: You took my pencil! I did not! Yes you did! Fine I'll just take yours then! Stop it both of you! I'm sorry, I didn't mean to take yours. Can I borrow it? Okay but ask next time. Sorry. It's okay. Let's get back to drawing.
notes: Mixed content - conflict present but fully resolved with SEL arc; tests nuanced scoring

description: Cooking show for kids - following instructions, patience
transcript: First we wash our hands! Safety first in the kitchen. Now we measure two cups of flour. Can you see the measuring cup? Pour it slowly into the bowl. Good job! Now we add one egg. Crack it gently on the edge. Oops I got some shell in there, let's fish it out. That's okay, mistakes happen when we cook! Now we stir it all together.
notes: Strong SEL mistake-tolerance modeling, practical vocabulary, age 4-7 ideal
